zh.lifeSummary: 當Isabel le Bigod於1210年11月1日出生在英國英格蘭諾福爾可特福德時，她的父親Hugh le Bigod, Earl of Norfolk24歲，母親Maud Matilda Marshall18歲。她於1234年4月12日與John FitzGeoffrey Lord of Shere and Justiciar of Ireland在英國英格蘭素瑞黑結婚。他們至少是4個兒子和10個女兒的父母。她去世於1258年11月23日在英國武斯特斯爾享年48歲，埋葬在英國英格蘭武斯特斯爾。

zh-hans.lifeSummary: 当Isabel le Bigod于1210年11月1日出生在英国英格兰諾福爾可特福德时，她的父亲Hugh le Bigod, Earl of Norfolk24岁，母亲Maud Matilda Marshall18岁。她于1234年4月12日与John FitzGeoffrey Lord of Shere and Justiciar of Ireland在英国英格兰素瑞黑结婚。他们至少是4个儿子和10个女儿的父母。她去世于1258年11月23日在英国武斯特斯爾享年48岁，埋葬在英国英格兰武斯特斯爾。

nameMeanings.name: le
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Vietnamese Thanh, Hung, Minh, Dung, Tuan, Hai, Hoa, Thuy, Son, Anh, Long, Hoang.  Chinese Hong, Sang, Chi, Dong, Tong, Lai, Hang, Man, Han, Ho, Phoung, Chan.  Korean Tam, Phong, Nam, Thai, Tuong, Chung, Thach, Tinh, Manh, Tham, Uyen, Vang.

Chinese: Mandarin form of the surname 樂, meaning ‘happiness’ in Chinese: from the first element of the personal name Le Fu (樂父), style name of Yan, son of the Duke Dai of Song (ruler of the state of Song, reigned 799–766  BC ). Yan's grandson Le Ju (樂莒) inherited Le (樂) as his surname.

Chinese: Mandarin form of the surname 勒: (i) from the second element of the placename Shu-Le (疏勒), an ancient state (located in present-day Xinjiang Uyghur autonomous region) during the Western Han dynasty (206  BC –25  AD ). (ii) a surname near the Bohai Sea region during the Han dynasty (206  BC –220  AD ). (iii) a surname traced back to the Hun ethnic group in northern China during the Jin dynasty (265–420  AD ).

variants.name: Yue
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Chinese: Chi, Feng, Gang, Wei, Chun, Guo, Jin, Kin, Ping, Yan, Yun, Zhang, Chau, Du, Hau, Hung, My, Sau.
Chinese:

Mandarin form of the surname 岳 (or 嶽), meaning ‘high mountain’ in Chinese: traced back to Si Yue (四岳 or 四嶽), said to be the title of an official post in charge of sacrificial rituals on the site of famous mountains during the reign of the legendary Emperor Yao (c. 24th century BC ) and Emperor Shun (c. 23rd century BC ).

variants.name: Lok
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Chinese Ping, Tat, Wing, Ying Lin, Chi Ming, Ching Wah, Chun, Chung Man, Han, Kam, Kam Tim, Kin.
Chinese: Cantonese form of the surname 樂, see   Le   1.
Chinese: Cantonese form of the surnames 陸, 鹿, 逯 and 禄, see   Lu   5 and (7-9).

variants.name: Li
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Chinese Wei, Ming, Li, Jian, Hong, Yan, Ping, Jing, Yong, Ying, Hui, Jin, Min, Chung.

Chinese: Mandarin form of the surname 李, meaning ‘plum’: (i) said to have been adopted in place of the Chinese surname 理 (also pronounced Li; meaning ‘rules’ in Chinese) by Li Zhen (利貞), son of an official called Li Zheng (理徵) in the late Shang dynasty (1600–1046  BC ). It is said that Li Zhen's father incurred the displeasure of King Zhou of Shang (died 1046  BC ) and Li Zhen fled to a wasteland area, where he survived on wild plums. After his return to civilization, he changed his surname to Li (李 ‘plum’), a word with the same pronunciation. The original surname, 理, is said to be from Li Guan (理官), the name of a post of officials in charge of legislation in ancient China. Li Er (李耳), also known as Lao Tzu or Lao Zi, traditionally regarded as the founder of Taoism, is believed to have been a descendant of Li Zhen. (ii) adopted as a surname by the Chi Li (叱李) family from the Xianbei ethnic group during the Northern Wei dynasty (386–534  AD ). (iii) in the early years of the Tang dynasty (618–907  AD ), sixteen high officials with different surnames were granted the surname Li (李), the surname of the royal family of the Tang dynasty, by Emperor Taizong of Tang (died 649  BC ), because of their contributions to the establishment of the Tang regime. (iv) the surname can also be traced back to several minority ethnic groups in ancient China.

Chinese: Mandarin form of the surname 黎, meaning ‘black’ or ‘multitude’ in ancient Chinese: (i) from Li (黎), the name of an ancient state (located in Changzhi in Shanxi province) during the Shang dynasty (1600–1046  BC ), which was annexed in the Western Zhou dynasty (1046–771  BC ) and later granted to Fengshu, also known as Li Hou (黎侯, 'Marquis Li'). (ii) adopted as a surname by the Su Li (素黎) family, members of the Xianbei ethnic group, during the Northern Wei dynasty (386–534  AD ).

nameMeanings.name: Bigod
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Polish Jaroslaw, Jozef, Krystyna, Slawomir.

Polish and Jewish (from Poland): from bigos, formerlydenoting anything that was chopped or slashed, now a particular dishcomposed of hash and sauerkraut; also ‘confusion’; hence perhaps anickname for someone who caused confusion.

nameMeanings.name: Isabel
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: Originally a Spanish version of   Elizabeth  , which was coined by deletion of the first syllable and alteration of the final consonant sound to one that can normally end a word in Spanish. The name was imported into France in the early Middle Ages, and thence into England. It was a royal name, and its enduring popularity may have been enhanced by the fact that it was borne by a queen of England—  Isabella  (  1296–1358 ), daughter of  Philip IV  of France—even though she led a turbulent life and eventually had her husband,  Edward II , murdered.

historicEvents.title: Magna Carta
historicEvents.description: King John of England agreed to a charter of rights called the Magna Carta. The document was originally drafted by the Archbishop of Canterbury to establish peace between King John and a group of rebel barons. The Magna Carta was completed in 1215 and is now considered to be one of the greatest constitutional documents of all time.

historicEvents.title: France Declares War on England
historicEvents.description: France declared war on England as Louis VIII of France attacked Poitou and northern Gascony.

en.lifeSummary: When Isabel le Bigod was born on 1 November 1210, in Thetford, Norfolk, England, United Kingdom, her father, Hugh le Bigod, Earl of Norfolk, was 24 and her mother, Maud Matilda Marshall, was 18. She married John FitzGeoffrey Lord of Shere and Justiciar of Ireland on 12 April 1234, in Shere, Surrey, England, United Kingdom. They were the parents of at least 4 sons and 10 daughters. She died on 23 November 1258, in Worcestershire, England, at the age of 48, and was buried in Worcestershire, England, United Kingdom.

fr.lifeSummary: Lorsque Isabel le Bigod est née le 1 novembre 1210, à Thetford, Norfolk, Angleterre, Royaume-Uni, son père, Hugh le Bigod, Earl of Norfolk, avait 24 ans et sa mère, Maud Matilda Marshall, avait 18 ans. Elle a épousé John FitzGeoffrey Lord of Shere and Justiciar of Ireland le 12 avril 1234, à Shere, Surrey, Angleterre, Royaume-Uni. Ils ont eu au moins 4 fils et 10 filles. Elle est décédée le 23 novembre 1258, à Worcestershire, Angleterre, à l’âge de 48 ans, et a été enterrée à Worcestershire, Angleterre, Royaume-Uni.

Lei sposato John FitzGeoffrey Lord of Shere and Justiciar of Ireland il 12 aprile 1234, nel Shere, Surrey, Inghilterra, Regno Unito. Erano genitori di almeno 4 figli e 10 figlie. Morì il 23 novembre 1258, nel Worcestershire, England, all'età di 48 anni, e fu sepolta a Worcestershire, Inghilterra, Regno Unito.

ja.lifeSummary: Isabel le Bigodが1210年11月1日にイギリスイギリスノーフォルクスエトフォードで生まれた時，父Hugh le Bigod, Earl of Norfolkは24歳，母Maud Matilda Marshallは18歳であった。1234年4月12日にイギリスイギリススリーシェアでJohn FitzGeoffrey Lord of Shere and Justiciar of Irelandと結婚した。彼らは少なくとも4人の息子と10人の娘の親であった。彼女は1258年11月23日にイギリスウオルセステルシュアイアで48歳で死去し，イギリスイギリスウオルセステルシュアイアにて埋葬された。

ko.lifeSummary: Isabel le Bigod(이)가 1210년 11월 1일 영국 잉글랜드 노르폴크 테트퍼드에서 태어났을 때, 그녀의 아버지 Hugh le Bigod, Earl of Norfolk(은)는 24세였고, 그녀의 어머니 Maud Matilda Marshall(은)는 18세였다. 그녀는 1234년 4월 12일 영국 잉글랜드 수리 셔에서 John FitzGeoffrey Lord of Shere and Justiciar of Ireland(와)과 결혼했다. 그들은 적어도 네 명의 아들과 열 명의 딸을 두었다. 그녀는 1258년 11월 23일 영국 워스스터실르에서 48세의 나이로 사망했으며 영국 잉글랜드 워스스터실르에 묻혔다.

Ela faleceu em 23 de novembro de 1258, em Worcestershire, Inglaterra, com 48 anos, e foi sepultada em Worcestershire, Inglaterra, United Kingdom.

ru.lifeSummary: Когда Isabel le Bigod родилась 1 ноября 1210 г. в местности тетфорд, норфолк, Англия, Великобритания, её отцу по имени Hugh le Bigod, Earl of Norfolk было 24 года, а матери, которую звали Maud Matilda Marshall, 18. Она вышла замуж за человека по имени John FitzGeoffrey Lord of Shere and Justiciar of Ireland 12 апреля 1234 г. в местности шир, сурри, Англия, Великобритания. У них было по крайней мере 4 сына и 10 дочерей. Она умерла 23 ноября 1258 г. в местности ворсестиршир, Англия в возрасте 48 лет и была похоронена в местности ворсестиршир, Англия, Великобритания.

es.1 November 1210–23 November 1258: 1 de noviembre de 1210–23 de noviembre de 1258
es.Tintern Abbey, Gloucestershire, England,: Tintern Abbey, Gloucestershire, Inglaterra, Reino Unido
es.lifeSummary: Cuando Isabel le Bigod nació el 1 de noviembre de 1210, en Thetford, Norfolk, Inglaterra, Reino Unido, su papá, Hugh le Bigod, Earl of Norfolk, tenía 24 años y su mamá, Maud Matilda Marshall, tenía 18 años. Se casó con John FitzGeoffrey Lord of Shere and Justiciar of Ireland el 12 de abril de 1234, en Shere, Surrey, Inglaterra, Reino Unido. Fueron padres de por lo menos 4 hijos y 10 hijas. Murió el 23 de noviembre de 1258, en Worcestershire, Inglaterra, a la edad de 48 años, y fue sepultada en Worcestershire, Inglaterra, Reino Unido.
